title: Find Peak Element
problem_statement: Given an array where nums[i] != nums[i+1], find a peak element and return its index. A peak element is greater than its neighbors.

examples.explanation: Either index with a local peak is acceptable.

intuition: Use binary search to move toward a rising slope. A peak exists where an element is larger than its neighbor.

approaches.name: Binary Search on Slope
approaches.description: If mid is lower than mid+1, a peak lies to the right; otherwise a peak lies to the left including mid.

interview_qa.q: Why is a peak guaranteed to exist?
interview_qa.a: The boundaries behave as if nums[-1] and nums[n] are -∞, so a peak must occur by the intermediate slope property.

interview_qa.q: Can either boundary be a peak?
interview_qa.a: Yes, a boundary can be a peak if it is greater than its single adjacent element.
